title: Fixing Broken HTML Table Structure with Missing Data Cells

statement.markdown: Write a function that takes an HTML table string as input and returns a string with the table structure validated and corrected. The function should ensure that each row has at least one data cell, and add a default cell if necessary. If the input table is empty, return an empty string.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing an HTML table, where each row is separated by a newline character.
statement.outputFormat: A string representing the validated and corrected HTML table.
statement.notes: You can assume that the input table is well-formed and does not contain any invalid HTML tags.

constraints.description: Maximum number of rows in the input table
Maximum number of columns in a row

examples.explanation: Input table has one row with one cell, no changes needed.
Input table has two rows, the second row is empty, so a default cell is added.

hints.text: Use a regex to extract rows and cells from the input table string
Iterate over each row and add a default cell if necessary

editorial.title: Fixing Broken HTML Table Structure
editorial.intuition: The key idea is to use a regex to extract rows and cells from the input table string, and then iterate over each row to add a default cell if necessary.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible approach is to parse the input table string using an HTML parser library, and then use a separate library to generate the corrected table string.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a regex to extract rows and cells, and then use a loop to add default cells where necessary.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution has a time complexity of O(n), where n is the number of rows in the input table.
commonMistakes: Not handling cases where a row has only one cell
Not adding a default cell when a row is empty
